canonical_name: a poor Northern chief who has suddenly Wakened to find himself High King of Britain
the wife of my most Christian of chiefs
no wench to tumble, and why in the name of all the Gods
a woman who is wedded to one of my most loyal partisans-
one of my most loyal partisans-
a man of lewd manners and little piety
that woman who had loved that strange man who bore the serpents on his arms in gold
one of your camp followers and fancy women
any man that way except your rightful husband
the people who look to you for help if the Saxons come
the travelling peddlers encamped in the marketplace
a woman to be given away without her own consent
a woman, with small knowledge of the world
that castle, which could be defended by only a few men
